game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **開店コンビニ日記** In Convenience Store Story, players manage and expand a convenience store by tapping on a grid-based layout to place shelves and display various products like "Onigiri," "Bento," "Sweets," and "Magazines." The core loop involves strategically arranging shelves to maximize customer flow and product visibility, stocking items that cater to customer preferences, and managing inventory to prevent stockouts. Players hire and train staff members, who improve their speed and efficiency in tasks like stocking shelves and operating the cash register as they gain experience, directly impacting store revenue and customer satisfaction. Progression is driven by earning profits to unlock new product categories, expand the store's physical footprint by purchasing adjacent land tiles, and fulfill specific customer requests to convert them into loyal regulars. The game features a multi-year calendar system where players must adapt to seasonal demands, negotiate partnerships with other companies for exclusive products, and eventually expand nationwide, competing against rival convenience stores to achieve the highest sales and customer satisfaction ratings in each region.
game_info.description: Welcome to Convenience Store Story! Start with a small shop and grow it into a thriving convenience store empire. Place shelves strategically, stock popular products, and serve customers efficiently. Hire staff to help manage operations, unlock new product categories as you earn profits, and expand your store footprint. Watch customer satisfaction and adapt to their preferences to build a successful business. Your goal is to reach $50,000 in total revenue while maintaining high customer satisfaction!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate cursor to select grid tiles or UI elements
Space: Confirm selection (place shelf, stock product, hire staff, or expand store)
Z: Cancel/Go back to previous menu
Shift: Speed up time (hold to accelerate game speed)
Enter: Start game from title screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart game (returns to title screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic game mechanics including placing shelves, stocking products, hiring staff, and serving customers. Validates that the core game loop functions correctly and that all basic interactions work as expected.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Places a few shelves near the entrance, stocks them with basic products (Onigiri and Bento), hires one staff member, and lets the game run to serve several customers. Tests navigation through menus and confirms all actions execute properly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test successfully places shelves, stocks products, hires staff, and serves multiple customers. Revenue increases over time, and the game runs without errors for at least 100 frames of active gameplay.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the winning condition by implementing an optimal strategy to reach $50,000 in revenue. This validates that the win condition is achievable and that the game progression works correctly through all stages.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Aggressively expands the store by placing many shelves in optimal positions near the entrance and checkout. Stocks high-margin products, hires multiple staff members to improve efficiency, and continuously restocks popular items. Uses time acceleration to speed up revenue generation.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The game reaches GAME_OVER_WIN state when total revenue exceeds $50,000. Customer satisfaction remains high throughout, staff efficiency improves over time, and all game mechanics contribute to achieving the win condition.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests store expansion mechanics by purchasing adjacent land tiles and placing shelves in the expanded area. Validates that expansion unlocks correctly and that customers can access the new areas.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Earns initial revenue, then purchases expansion tiles systematically. Places shelves in expanded areas and verifies customers visit these new locations. Tests boundary conditions and ensures expanded areas integrate seamlessly with the original store.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Successfully unlocks and purchases multiple expansion tiles. Shelves placed in expanded areas function identically to original areas. Customers navigate to and purchase from expanded sections, contributing to overall revenue.
